Version.Name: Developmental Toxicity model (CAESAR)
Version.Summary: QSAR classification model for Developmental Toxicity (from CAESAR project)

Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction of developmental toxicity, based on a binary classification of FDA criteria (FDA categories A and B are considered as non toxicant, categories C, D and X are considered toxicant) . It is implemented inside the VEGA online platform, accessible at:http://www.vega-qsar.eu/ The model extends the original CAESAR model, freely available at: http://www.caesar-project.eu/software/
Guide.Model: The model is a QSAR classification model based on a Random Forest method, implemented using WEKA open-source libraries. Full reference and details of the used formulas can be found in: Cassano, A., Manganaro, A., Martin, T., Young, D., Piclin, N., Pintore, M., Bigoni, D. and Benfenati, E. (2010). CAESAR models for developmental toxicity., Chem Cent J 4 Suppl 1, S4
Guide.Descriptors: The descriptors used by the Random Forest model are the following: - SdssC: sum of all ( ≡ C – ) E-State values in molecule - SHssNH: sum of all [ – NH – ] E-State values in molecule - icycem: mean information on the vertex cycle matrix equality - BEHm1: highest eigenvalue n.1 of Burden matrix / weighted by atomic masses - BELv1: lowest eigenvalue n. 1 of Burden matrix / weighted by atomic van der Waals volumes - BELv8: lowest eigenvalue n.8 of Burden matrix / weighted by atomic van der Waals volumes - BELp3: lowest eigenvalue n.3 of Burden matrix / weighted by atomic polarizabilities - MATS4v: Moran autocorrelation - lag4 / weighted by atomic van der Waals volumes - MATS1p: Moran autocorrelation - lag1 / weighted by atomic polarizabilities - MATS4p: Moran autocorrelation - lag4 / weighted by atomic polarizabilities - GATS2m: Geary autocorrelation - lag 2/ weighted by atomic polarizabilities - GATS3v: Geary autocorrelation - lag 3 / weighted by atomic van der Waals volumes - GATS1p: Geary autocorrelation - lag1 / weighted by atomic polarizabilities The descriptors are calculated by an in-house JAVA software, developed by Todd Martin (EPA), based on the CDK open-source libraries , as developed for the original CAESAR model.

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model
